response: [The indigenous community member is known for their expertise in tracking elephant herds.] [They have spent many years studying the patterns and behaviors of elephants in the reserve.] [Today, they are observing a group of elephants at a nearby waterhole.] [This waterhole is a vital source of water for the elephants in the area.] [It is a place where elephants often gather to drink, rest, and interact.] [The indigenous community member has noticed that three different herds have come together at the waterhole.] [This is an unusual occurrence, as elephants usually travel in smaller groups or remain in their own herds.] [The first herd consists of 12 elephants.] [This is the smallest of the three herds that have gathered today.] [The second herd is twice as large as the first herd.] [To calculate the size of the second herd, one must multiply the number of elephants in the first herd by two.] [Twelve multiplied by two equals twenty-four.] [So, the second herd has 24 elephants.] [The third herd is 5 elephants smaller than the second herd.] [To determine the size of the third herd, one must subtract 5 from the number of elephants in the second herd.] [Twenty-four minus five equals nineteen.] [Therefore, the third herd consists of 19 elephants.] [Now, to find the total number of elephants at the waterhole, one must add the sizes of all three herds.] [Adding the first herd, second herd, and third herd together will give the total number.] [The first herd has 12 elephants.] [The second herd has 24 elephants.] [The third herd has 19 elephants.] [Adding 12 and 24 gives 36.] [Then, adding 19 to 36 gives a total of 55.] [So, there are 55 elephants in total at the waterhole.] [This number includes all the elephants from the three herds that have gathered.] [The indigenous community member is carefully observing the elephants to ensure their safety.] [They are also noting any signs of stress or discomfort among the elephants.] [The presence of multiple herds at the waterhole may indicate a change in the environment.] [It could be due to a shortage of water or food in other areas.] [The elephants are drawn to the waterhole for essential resources.]
